name: Optiopia
description: make an RPG called Optiopia with 3 gods that created the world, open world, towns with npcs, inventory system, player starts with sword wand or bow

conversation.content: make an RPG called Optiopia with 3 gods that created the world, open world, towns with npcs, inventory system, player starts with sword wand or bow

What is the specific lore or back story of the three gods who created the world in Optiopia?: One god was cooking diner for the other two gods and while making the food mixed in some ingredianets they dont normally use and it ended up making a superior dish the likes no god had ever seen before. The gods loved the food so much they made demigods/demihumans and a planet full of resources so they could experiance new flavors and tastes they never knew existed the demigods got bored of farming so they made humans and now the humans live everyday worshiping the gods and farming the land blisfully unaware of there freedom they were granted on the planet. One day the player wakes up and realises they dont have to give everything they make to the gods, the players have free will. The player then has to fight monsters, explore dungeons, complete quests, etc to get ingredients to make the best food the gods have ever seen. The food will power up the hero/player with bonuses and special attacks/powers you can fill in the gaps for the story.
How will the inventory system integrate with the game's mechanics and player progression?: yes to inventory no player progreesion for this project

right now it asks me to choose a class in the powershell, all options and selections should be done in the game window, the game also crashes when I start it so lets debug it and get everything working

1. **Check for Updates**: Ensure that both your operating system and the game are up to date. Sometimes, crashes are caused by bugs that have been fixed in newer versions.

2. **Compatibility Settings**: If you're using Windows, try running the game in compatibility mode. Right-click on the game's executable file, select "Properties," go to the "Compatibility" tab, and check "Run this program in compatibility mode for:" Then choose an older version of Windows that is known to work with the game.

3. **System Requirements**: Make sure your PC meets the minimum system requirements for the game. If not, consider upgrading your hardware components like RAM or GPU.

4. **Graphics Driver**: Update your graphics drivers to the latest version. Sometimes, outdated drivers can cause games to crash or run poorly.

5. **Disable Antivirus/Firewall Temporarily**: While it's generally not recommended, temporarily disabling your antivirus software or firewall might help identify if they are causing the issue.

6. **Check for Corrupted Files**: Reinstall the game to ensure there are no corrupted files. Sometimes, incomplete downloads or installations can cause crashes.

7. **Run as Administrator**: Right-click on the game's executable file and select "Run as administrator." This might resolve permission-related issues that could be causing the crash.

8. **Check Event Viewer**: On Windows, you can check the Event Viewer for any error messages related to the game crash. This can provide more insight into what might be going wrong.

9. **Memory Issues**: If your system is running low on RAM, it might cause games to crash. Close unnecessary programs and try running the game again.

10. **Contact Support or Community Forums**: If

It sounds like you're describing an issue where your game is trying to use PowerShell for character selection instead of using the game's graphical user interface (GUI). This can be frustrating because it disrupts the immersion and functionality of the game.

Here are some steps you can take to resolve this issue:

1. **Check Game Settings**: 
   - Open the game's settings menu.
   - Look for an option related to "Graphics" or "Display".
   - Ensure that the setting for "Use GUI" or "Use On-Screen Interface" is enabled.

2. **Update the Game**:
   - Make sure your game is up-to-date. Developers often release patches that fix bugs and improve compatibility with different systems.
   - Go to the game's official website or platform (e.g., Steam, Epic Games Store) and check for updates.

3. **Run as Administrator**:
   - Right-click on the game executable file.
   - Select "Run as administrator".
   - Sometimes, running the game with elevated privileges can resolve issues related to interface rendering.

4. **Check Compatibility Mode**:
   - If you're using Windows, right-click on the game's shortcut.
   - Go to "Properties" > "Compatibility".
   - Try enabling or disabling different compatibility modes (e.g., Windows 7, Windows 8) and see if that resolves the issue.

5. **Reinstall the Game**:
   - Uninstall the game completely from your system.
   - Re-download it from the official source.
   - Install it again and check if the issue persists.

6. **Contact Support**:
   - If none of the above steps work, consider reaching out to the game's support team for assistance.
   - Provide them with detailed information about the problem, including your operating system, game version, and any error messages you might be seeing.

If the issue continues despite these steps, it may be a bug specific to your setup or hardware. In

world.premise: In Optiopia, three gods created a world filled with resources and demigods/demihumans to explore new flavors. Humans were made to farm and worship these gods. The player wakes up discovering free will and embarks on quests to find the best ingredients for divine dishes.

lore.creation_myth: The three gods created the world while cooking a dish. An ingredient mix-up resulted in an unparalleled meal, leading to the creation of demigods/demihumans and humans who farm resources.
major_events: Demigods' boredom led to human creation
Player's realization of free will sets off quests
lore.ancient_history: The gods established a balanced ecosystem for food exploration, ensuring resources were abundant.

factions.name: Harvesters Guild
factions.description: A group of humans dedicated to farming and gathering.
factions.goals: Maximize crop yields and resource collection.

factions.name: Shadowspike Cult
factions.description: A secretive faction seeking forbidden knowledge.
factions.goals: Uncover ancient secrets to gain power.

gods_or_powers.name: Aetheria
gods_or_powers.description: The god of rejuvenation, associated with the mystic grove.

gods_or_powers.name: Terrestrialus
gods_or_powers.description: The god of stability, linked to crystal tower.

gods_or_powers.name: Pyroflux
gods_or_powers.description: The god of culinary arts, connected to aurora fields.

rules: Players must gather unique ingredients from various regions.
Cooking specific recipes grants divine powers and bonuses.
limitations: Ingredients are rare and hard to find.

towns.name: Aurorastead
towns.description: A bustling town with a focus on culinary arts.

threats.name: Gorgon Glimmer
threats.description: A terrifying monster with a petrifying gaze.

threats.name: Blight of Shadowspike
threats.description: An ancient curse affecting crops and livestock.

mechanics.core_loop: The player explores the open world of Optiopia, interacts with NPCs, fights monsters, completes quests, and collects ingredients to create superior dishes for the gods.

rules: Players can choose between sword, wand, or bow as their starting weapon.
Combat involves managing HP and MP while selecting actions to defeat enemies.

starting_options.name: Warrior
starting_options.description: A strong and resilient fighter equipped with a sword.

starting_options.name: Mage
starting_options.description: A powerful spellcaster using the might of magic through a wand.

starting_options.name: Ranger
starting_options.description: An agile archer skilled in ranged combat with a bow.

enemies.name: Orc
enemies.behavior: Charges towards the nearest player and attempts to grapple them.

enemies.name: Witch
enemies.behavior: Casts spells that reduce player stats before attacking.

mechanics.difficulty_scaling: Enemies' stats increase linearly with the player's level, ensuring a balanced challenge throughout the game.
